eventMetadata.context_description: EDward Gaming and SYGaming advanced to Round 2 of the Honor of Kings Challenger Cup 2026 Stage 1 single-elimination bracket via contrasting Bo5 wins: EDG's efficient 3-1 defeat of lower-tier QingXun-QianYuan on April 25, and SYG's resilient 3-2 comeback over blessing-aided ZDC on April 26. EDG carries KPL pedigree with recent mixed TY TianJiao Cup showings (wins over Team WE and Hero JiuJing, losses to Wolves and Weibo Gaming), supported by stable roster including junglers XiaLing and YunLi. SYGaming rides KGL Spring group stage momentum as second-place finishers. In this pivotal Bo7, trader focus centers on EDG's macro execution versus SYG's upset potential through extended series endurance and draft flexibility.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Democrat Analilia Mejia's commanding trader consensus at 95.7% odds for a 20-25% margin of victory in New Jersey's 11th Congressional District special election stems from certified vote tallies reported after the April 16 contest, showing her at 60.2% (81,825 votes) to Republican Joe Hathaway's 39.4% (53,520 votes), a 20.8-point edge that outperformed 2024 presidential margins in the Democratic-leaning district. Pre-election polling (e.g., GBAO March survey: Mejia 53%, Hathaway 36%) and her progressive endorsements from figures like Bernie Sanders and AOC bolstered expectations of a hold on the seat vacated by Gov. Mikie Sherrill. With AP and media calls finalized and no recounts announced, outstanding provisional or mail ballots pose the main—though improbable—risk of dipping below 20%, as late counts typically favor Democrats in low-turnout specials. Certification expected soon could resolve the market.

markets.question: Will both candidates advancing to the general election for Governor of California from the June 2, 2026 primary be affiliated with the Republican Party?
markets.description:
The non-partisan primary election for Governor of California is scheduled to take place on June 2, 2026. The top two candidates in this election by number of votes won will advance to the general election for Governor of California.

This market will resolve according to the party affiliation of the listed candidates who advance from the primary to the general election for Governor of California. A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with a party.

In case any combination of parties advances that is not listed here, including all scenarios where an independent candidate advances, this market will resolve to "Other".

If no 2026 California gubernatorial primary takes place by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the results of the primary election for Governor of California as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the government of California, specifically the Office of the Secretary of State.

eventMetadata.context_description: In California's nonpartisan top-two primary on June 2, trader consensus favors a Democrat-Republican matchup at 74% implied probability, driven by late April polls showing former Attorney General Xavier Becerra surging to 21-24% after Rep. Eric Swalwell's April 11 withdrawal amid sexual assault allegations, which consolidated Democratic support without creating a runaway leader. Republican Steve Hilton polls close behind at 16-23% across aggregates like Decision Desk HQ, bolstered by former President Trump's endorsement, while Riverside County Sheriff Chad Bianco holds 13-14%; the fragmented Democratic field—including Tom Steyer at 14-17% and Katie Porter at 10%—prevents a Dem-Dem sweep (15%), and low Rep-Rep odds (2.9%) reflect insufficient combined GOP strength. Mail ballots begin soon, with final debates potentially tipping undecideds (20-26%).

markets.question: MegaETH FDV above $700M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of MegaETH's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

Only an official token launched by MegaETH will qualify. Stablecoins, memecoins, LSTs and synthetic tokens will not count.

The token must be actively and publicly tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If MegaETH doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Former U.S. Rep. Ben McAdams leads trader consensus at 73% implied probability for Utah's 1st Congressional District Democratic primary on June 23, driven by his top polling (36% in late March Data for Progress survey), superior fundraising ($1.5 million raised, $863,000 cash on hand), and prior name recognition from his 2019-2021 House service and Salt Lake County mayoralty. State Sen. Nate Blouin's 25% share reflects progressive endorsements like Bernie Sanders' but is weighed down by mid-April resurfacing of decade-old offensive social media posts mocking Mormons and joking about sexual assault. Liban Mohamed's narrow ranked-choice convention endorsement win on April 25 boosted activists but failed to sway broader primary odds, as delegates differ from voters; low-fundraised challengers like Caroline Gleich trail. No new polls have emerged post-convention.

markets.question: Will Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) hit (LOW) $300 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Tesla's share price trades around $381 as of May 1, 2026, reflecting trader optimism following Q1 earnings on April 22 that beat adjusted EPS expectations at 41 cents versus 37 cents forecast, with revenue up 16% year-over-year to $22.39 billion despite a deliveries miss. Elevated automotive margins and a 25% increase in 2026 capital expenditure guidance to over $25 billion—targeted at AI infrastructure, robotaxis, and Optimus robotics—have bolstered sentiment, positioning Tesla as an AI stalwart per analysts like Wedbush ($600 price target) and Morgan Stanley ($415). Consensus analyst targets average near $400, with key May catalysts including Full Self-Driving software updates and unsupervised robotaxi scaling potentially driving volatility amid EV demand headwinds and competition from BYD.

markets.question: Mobile Legends Bang Bang: EVOS vs Alter Ego (BO3) - MPL Indonesia Regular Season
markets.description:
This market refers to the Mobile Legends Bang Bang match between EVOS and Alter Ego in the MPL Indonesia Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 2 at 3:15AM ET.

This market will resolve to "EVOS" if EVOS win the match against Alter Ego.

This market will resolve to "Alter Ego" if Alter Ego win the match against EVOS.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In MPL Indonesia Season 17 regular season, Alter Ego and EVOS clash in a pivotal Week 6 BO3 rematch on May 2, with both mid-table teams battling for playoff positioning—AE at 5-4 (5th place, -1 map differential) and EVOS at 4-5 (6th, even maps). AE holds the edge from their 2-1 Week 2 victory, fueled by aggressive jungler Yazukee and goldlaner Arfy's carry potential, and enters off a gritty 2-1 comeback win over NAVI last week. EVOS, meanwhile, stumbles on a two-series skid with 0-2 losses to top contenders ONIC and Bigetron by Vitality, despite earlier sweeps. Expect macro-focused drafts emphasizing EXP laner Nino's impact for AE in this double round-robin grinder.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Brandon Ingram, James Harden, and Jayson Tatum virtually even as frontrunners for Eastern Conference Finals MVP, reflecting intense competition among the Cavaliers, Raptors, and Celtics to emerge from tightly contested first-round series into potential ECF matchups. Cleveland's 125-120 Game 5 victory over Toronto yesterday—fueled by Harden's 23 points and nine rebounds alongside Donovan Mitchell's scoring—gave the Cavs a 3-2 edge despite Ingram's consistent 20+ point outputs and Scottie Barnes' two-way impact for the resilient Raptors. Tatum's playoff-leading averages of 24.7 points, 9.3 rebounds, and 8.4 assists per game post-Achilles recovery have powered Boston's tie with Philadelphia, while New York Knicks' sweep of Atlanta elevates Jalen Brunson but leaves the bracket path fluid, sustaining the star trio's deadlock amid rest advantages and home-court factors.

events.title: Which artists will have #1 hits in the US in May?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any song by the listed artist is in the #1 spot on the Spotify Top 50 - USA chart for any day of the relevant month, ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only primary artist profiles will qualify; features or collaborations under another artist profile will not qualify to resolve the featured artist to "Yes".

The resolution source for this market will be Spotify, specifically the Spotify Top 50 - USA chart, which can be found here: https://open.spotify.com/playlist/37i9dQZEVXbLRQDuF5jeBp.

eventMetadata.context_description: Olivia Rodrigo has surged to the top of trader sentiment after "Drop Dead" debuted at No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 chart dated May 2, securing the first #1 hit of May via explosive streaming, sales, and early radio support amid hype for her next album. This ends Ella Langley's impressive seven-week reign with "Choosin' Texas," highlighting pop's volatile chart dynamics where viral moments and playlist adds drive rapid shifts. For the remaining weeks (May 9, 16, 23, 30), competition intensifies from Drake's anticipated release strategy, Bad Bunny's Latin crossover strength, Taylor Swift's proven multi-week dominance, and surprises like Noah Kahan or Don Toliver. Watch midweek tracking data, Spotify streams, and TikTok virality as key swing factors before Tuesday chart drops.

markets.question: Will Zelenskyy post 0-19 posts from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Volodymyr Zelenskyy (@ZelenskyyUa), posts on X between May 1, 12:00 PM ET and May 8, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Abstract FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Abstract's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Abstract doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will Silver (SI) settle over $75 on the final trading day of June 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official CME settlement price for the Active Month of Silver futures on the final trading day of June 2026 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Silver (SI) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (March, May, July, September, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month becomes a non-active month effective on its First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days during June on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Silver (SI) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Silver futures (SI) trade near $74.50 per ounce as of May 1, 2026, reflecting trader consensus on persistent supply deficits—now in their sixth year—and robust industrial demand from solar photovoltaics, electronics, and EVs, which comprise over 60% of total consumption per Silver Institute data. A 148% surge through 2025 has given way to consolidation, with prices up modestly 0.11% today amid elevated geopolitical risks and Fed policy uncertainty boosting safe-haven flows. Divergent forecasts abound: J.P. Morgan eyes a $81/oz 2026 average, while Bank of America cites squeeze risks for $135–$309 peaks. Key catalysts include mid-May CPI, June 6 nonfarm payrolls, and the June FOMC meeting, where rate cut odds could pressure the U.S. dollar and lift precious metals if softer data emerges.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the IL-09 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the IL-09 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Illinois' 9th Congressional District, a solidly Democratic seat with a Cook Partisan Voting Index of D+19, favors the party nominee following Rep. Jan Schakowsky's 2025 retirement announcement and the March 17, 2026 primaries that produced Evanston Mayor Daniel Biss as Democratic standard-bearer after a crowded 16-way contest and pastor John Elleson as Republican nominee. Biss's victory, backed by strong local name recognition, prior state Senate service, and primary fundraising dominance exceeding $12 million district-wide, has solidified trader consensus at 92.5% for Democratic Party retention amid no general election polling showing competitiveness. While a GOP midterm national wave, Biss scandal, health issue, or turnout collapse in this safe blue stronghold could shift odds before the November 3 general election, structural district math and historical incumbent-party holds in D+19 seats underpin the commanding position.

markets.question: Will Jerome Powell depart as Fed Chair by June 30 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Jerome Powell vacates his role as Chair of the Federal Reserve by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Vacating his role refers to Powell actually ceasing to hold the position of Chair of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors. Announcements of resignations or firings will not alone qualify. 

The scheduled end of Powell’s term as Chair will not alone qualify. If Powell continues to serve as Chair on a temporary basis (e.g. until the confirmation of his successor), he will not be considered to have vacated his role as Chair.

This market is restricted to Powell’s status as Chair of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors. Powell’s status as a regular member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors will not affect this market’s resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Jerome Powell and the Federal Reserve; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jerome Powell's term as Federal Reserve Chair expires on May 15, 2026, after which he has confirmed plans to step down from the leadership role but remain on the Board of Governors until January 2028, citing his ongoing governor term and a recently concluded Justice Department probe cleared on April 25. President Trump nominated former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh as successor, with the Senate Banking Committee advancing his confirmation process amid Republican support from figures like Sen. Thom Tillis; a full Senate vote could occur soon, potentially aligning Powell's departure with term end or shortly after. The Federal Open Market Committee's April 29-30 meeting, likely Powell's last as chair, held interest rates steady amid economic stabilization signals, shaping trader focus on swift leadership transition and Warsh's policy stance on rate cuts.

markets.question: Butvilas vs. Rehberg: Set 1 Games O/U 10.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Edas Butvilas and Max Hans Rehberg in the Shymkent 2, originally scheduled for May 1, 2026 at 2:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed in the first set equals or exceeds 11. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
Any first-set tiebreak counts as one (1) game toward the total.
If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins, or delayed beyond 7 days without commencement, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official Challenger results.
markets.groupItemTitle: Shymkent 2: Edas Butvilas vs Max Hans Rehberg Set 1 O/U 10.5

events.title: Shymkent 2: Edas Butvilas vs Max Hans Rehberg
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Edas Butvilas and Max Hans Rehberg in the Shymkent 2, originally scheduled for May 1, 2026 at 2:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Edas Butvilas' if Edas Butvilas advances against Max Hans Rehberg.

This market will resolve to 'Max Hans Rehberg' if Max Hans Rehberg advances against Edas Butvilas.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the ATP Challenger Shymkent 2 quarterfinals on Kazakhstan's outdoor clay courts, top seed Edas Butvilas (ATP No. 261) faces qualifier Max Hans Rehberg (No. 641) with no prior head-to-head. Butvilas, 21, advanced via dominant straight-sets wins over Zangar Nurlanuly (6-0, 6-3) and Gerard Campana Lee (7-5, 6-1) yesterday, building on his 6-5 clay record this year. Rehberg, 22, rides momentum from qualies and main-draw upsets, crushing higher-ranked Sergey Fomin (6-2, 6-0) and Sean Cuenin (7-6(7), 6-2) in the last 48 hours, showcasing clay prowess (54-52 career). Mild weather (around 80°F) poses no delays; trader sentiment weighs Butvilas's ranking edge against Rehberg's hot streak.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Karachi be 33°C on May 2?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Masroor Airbase Station in degrees Celsius on 2 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Masroor Airbase Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/pk/karachi/OPKC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Market-implied probabilities cluster around 37°C (34.5%) and 38°C (36.5%) for Karachi's highest temperature at Masroor Airbase Station on May 2, reflecting Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) forecasts of hot, dry conditions with maximums in that range amid a persisting heatwave from late April. Global models like those aggregated on AccuWeather project peaks near 39°C under hazy sunshine and afternoon breezes, but ensemble spread introduces uncertainty, with sea breeze onset potentially capping intensity at 37°C versus stronger continental heating pushing 38°C or higher. May climatological highs average 35°C; current high-pressure ridge and low humidity favor above-normal readings. Watch PMD's midday update and real-time observations for shifts, as 1-2°C model divergences highlight short-term forecast variability.

markets.question: Will Tesla (TSLA) close above $370 on May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) on May 1 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If the final session is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance. The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/TSLA/history, published under "Historical Prices."

Primary outcomes, turnout dynamics, and national midterm trends could tip the balance post-June.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cleveland Cavaliers hold an 88.8% implied probability to win their first-round NBA playoffs series against the Toronto Raptors after rallying for a 125-120 Game 5 victory on April 30, taking a commanding 3-2 lead with Game 6 set for Scotiabank Arena. Toronto's offense has crumbled without starting point guard Immanuel Quickley, out for the series with a hamstring injury, while Brandon Ingram exited Game 5 with a heel issue and Scottie Barnes nurses a quad strain, severely limiting spacing and playmaking against Cleveland's stout defense anchored by Evan Mobley and Jarrett Allen. The Cavs, largely healthy per recent injury reports with only minor concerns like Sam Merrill's hamstring, boast home-court advantage for a potential Game 7 and momentum from Donovan Mitchell and James Harden's clutch performances, reflecting trader consensus on their path to advancement despite the Raptors' gritty home win in Game 4.

markets.question: Will xAI have the best AI model at the end of June 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company which owns the model which has the highest arena rank based off the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on June 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" section on the Leaderboard tab of https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with the style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns Anthropic a 71% implied probability of fielding the top large language model by June 30, 2026, per the LMSYS Chatbot Arena leaderboard, driven by Claude Opus 4.7 Thinking's recent dominance in April 2026 benchmarks, where it outpaced rivals in crowdsourced Elo ratings for reasoning, coding, and real-world tasks. Anthropic's aggressive release cadence—major updates like Opus 4.6 and 4.7 since February—has widened its lead over OpenAI's GPT-5.5 (launched April 23) and Google's Gemini 3.1, reflecting superior scaling and automated alignment innovations. Google holds 20% as traders anticipate a hinted Gemini 3.5 or 4.0 soon, while others lag amid competitive moats. Watch for pre-June model drops or leaderboard shifts as key catalysts.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Recent bipartisan introductions, such as the April 28 CHATBOT Act for child safety on AI chatbots and the American Leadership in AI Act, target narrow issues like deepfakes and parental controls but lack the comprehensive safety testing or risk reporting required for market resolution and remain stalled in committees. With states enacting their own AI laws amid federal inaction, traders see slim odds of floor votes, passage, or presidential signature by year-end.

markets.question: Will monthly inflation increase by ≥1.1% in April?
markets.description:
This is a market about the one-month percent change in the seasonally adjusted Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) published by the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS).

This market will resolve to the one-month percent change in the seasonally adjusted Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) in April 2026 according to the monthly BLS report.

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index report released for April 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm), currently scheduled to be released on May 12, 2026, at 8:30 AM ET. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) which BLS reports to one decimal point (e.g. 0.4%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

If the BLS does not release the relevant figures on the scheduled date, this market may remain open up until the scheduled release time of the next CPI report (https://www.bls.gov/schedule). If the information is not released by that time, this market will resolve according to the figures of the most recent previous month with available data.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tight uncertainty in forecast models for Ankara's May 2 high temperature, with 8°C leading at 35% implied probability amid an Arctic airmass driven by a persistent omega block pattern funneling northerly cold air into central Turkey. The Turkish State Meteorological Service projects a high of 11°C under partly cloudy skies with light winds, while the UK Met Office sees 8°C with drizzle and gusts to 21 mph, highlighting model divergences on cloud cover thickness, precipitation timing, and cold advection strength. These factors differentiate outcomes: thicker low clouds could cap highs at 7°C, while clearer skies might push toward 9-10°C. New ECMWF and GFS runs expected within 24 hours could refine probabilities as the cold snap peaks.

markets.question: Will Jorge Rodríguez be the leader of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Nicolás Maduro as the leading Venezuela head of state by end-2026 at 65%, despite his January 3 U.S. capture and ongoing narcoterrorism trial in New York, where a March judge declined dismissal motions but proceedings drag amid funding disputes. Uncertainty over Venezuela's constitution—capping vice presidential interim at 90 days (extendable to 180, now exceeded without National Assembly declaration of absolute absence triggering elections)—fuels bets on Maduro's potential return via plea or release. Acting president Delcy Rodríguez holds 17.5% implied probability after U.S. sanctions lift (April 1), minimum income hikes to $240 (April 30), and first U.S. commercial flights resumption (May 1). Opposition leader María Corina Machado's 8.5% reflects her April 20 pledge for year-end return and swift polls amid rival factions. Key catalysts ahead: July absence deadline, IMF negotiations.

markets.question: Theo FDV above $1B one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Theo's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Theo (https://x.com/Theo_Network) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will SpaceX's market cap be between $1.8T and $2.0T at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

events.title: SpaceX IPO Closing Market Cap (Lower Strikes)
events.description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket heavily favors a SpaceX IPO closing market cap exceeding $2 trillion at 57% implied probability, propelled by the company's confidential S-1 filing in early April 2026 and subsequent Bloomberg reports boosting the target valuation above $2 trillion from an initial $1.75 trillion. This reflects SpaceX's dominance in reusable Falcon 9 launches—over 100 missions last year—coupled with Starlink's satellite constellation surpassing 7 million subscribers and generating billions in recurring revenue, alongside Starship's rapid test cadence toward orbital refueling. Recent disclosures tie Elon Musk's compensation to Mars milestones, underscoring long-term multi-planetary ambitions amid AI-driven data center satellite plans. Traders eye the late-May prospectus release and early-June roadshow as key catalysts, with resolution looming by June 28, though regulatory scrutiny and execution risks linger.

markets.question: Will Colorado Rapids win the 2026 MLS Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins the 2026 MLS Cup. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the 2026 MLS Cup per the rules of MLS (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 MLS season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Major League Soccer; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Inter Miami CF as the slim MLS Cup 2026 favorite at 17.5% implied probability, buoyed by Lionel Messi's star power, an eight-match unbeaten streak, and road dominance (5W-1D-1L away) despite home draws at new Nu Stadium, including a recent 1-1 vs. New England Revolution. Vancouver Whitecaps and LAFC trail closely at 11.5% each, reflecting Vancouver's league-best +20 goal difference and near-perfect 8-1-0 start (latest 3-1 over Colorado Rapids), alongside LAFC's recovery with a 1-0 win at Minnesota after stumbles. The bunched top reflects early-season parity after ~10 matches, with West leaders San Jose Earthquakes (9-1-0, 27 points) surging via Timo Werner's April Player of the Month form and Nashville SC topping the East at 22 points—highlighting MLS's long 34-game grind, playoff expansion, and upset potential keeping the race wide open.

markets.question: Will NYC Mayor post 0-19 posts from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani (@NYCMayor), posts on X between May 1, 12:00 PM ET and May 8, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jasmine Clark's trader consensus at 80.5% implied probability in the GA-13 Democratic primary reflects her commanding position following incumbent Rep. David Scott's death last week, which transformed the May 19 contest into an open-seat race. As a state representative who had surged to tie Scott in pre-death polling, Clark boasts the strongest fundraising—outpacing rivals in recent FEC reports—and benefits from over $580,000 in recent super PAC support from Protect Progress. Early voting began April 27 amid a recent candidate debate, bolstering her momentum in this DeKalb-Gwinnett battleground. Everton Blair Jr. holds second at 15% on local endorsements like DeKalb County DA Sherry Boston, positioning him for a potential runoff if no candidate secures a majority, while others trail amid fragmented fields.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the WI-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the WI-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Glenn Grothman’s strong track record in the R+8 Wisconsin 6th Congressional District, where he won 61% in 2024 amid Donald Trump’s 58% district margin, anchors trader consensus at 81% for a Republican victory. The crowded Democratic primary field of eight candidates—including Amanda Bell and Bradley Smith—threatens to produce a fragmented, underfunded nominee, with the top Democrat holding just $53,000 cash on hand versus Grothman’s $700,000. Independents Michael Thurow and Elizabeth Fitzgibbon pose minimal threats in this safe Republican seat per Cook Political Report and Sabato’s Crystal Ball ratings. Recent Grothman legislative wins, like his Federal Fraud Prevention bill advancing April 30, reinforce incumbency advantages ahead of the June 1 filing deadline and August 11 primaries.

markets.question: Jimmy Lai released by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Jimmy Lai is released from custody by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Lai is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Lai is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Lai to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Extradition of Lai to another country where he remains in custody will also not count.

Temporary outings from prison for purposes such as testifying in court, while still under the custody of correctional authorities, will not count as a release.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from relevant government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong pro-democracy media tycoon Jimmy Lai's 20-year prison sentence under the national security law, handed down on February 9, 2026, for collusion with foreign forces—the harshest penalty to date—anchors trader consensus at 97% against release by June 30. His legal team's March confirmation that he will not appeal the conviction eliminates key procedural paths to freedom, while April government efforts to seize $16 million in assets signal ongoing crackdown. At age 78, Lai faces an effectively life term amid Beijing's tightened control over dissent. Rare scenarios like acute health deterioration or executive pardon could alter odds, though none appear imminent.

markets.question: Game Handicap: TEX (-2.5) vs Toronto KOI (+2.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Call of Duty match between Toronto KOI and OpTic Texas in the Call of Duty League Stage 3 Major Qualifiers Qualifiers, initially scheduled for May 2 at 7:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "OpTic Texas" if OpTic Texas wins 3 or more games than Toronto KOI in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Toronto KOI". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.
markets.groupItemTitle: Game Handicap: TEX (-2.5) vs Toronto KOI (+2.5)

events.title: Call of Duty: Toronto KOI vs OpTic Texas (BO5) - Call of Duty League Stage 3 Major Qualifiers Qualifiers
events.description:
This market refers to the Call of Duty match between Toronto KOI and OpTic Texas in the Call of Duty League Stage 3 Major Qualifiers Qualifiers, initially scheduled for May 2 at 7:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Toronto KOI" if Toronto KOI win the match against OpTic Texas.

This market will resolve to "OpTic Texas" if OpTic Texas win the match against Toronto KOI.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: OpTic Texas, the CDL standings leader with 310 points, holds trader consensus edge over Toronto KOI (7th, 160 points) in their high-stakes BO5 Stage 3 Major Qualifiers matchup on May 2, driven by a dominant 3-1 elimination win over KOI in the April Minor I tournament and superior scrim results. Both teams remain unbeaten in qualifiers—Toronto 2-0 series (6-2 maps), OpTic 1-0 (3-1 maps)—positioning the winner for Major bracket advancement amid the Black Ops 6 meta favoring OpTic's elite hardpoint efficiency (top win rate). Toronto's mid-season addition of Kips bolsters their roster rebound, but slayer inconsistencies, especially around Insight, persist as a vulnerability, while no roster changes or patches alter the landscape.

markets.question: Will Bristol City FC vs. Stoke City FC end in a draw?

In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 2, 2026

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.description: This event is for the upcoming EFL Championship game, scheduled for Saturday, May 2, 2026 between Bristol City FC and Stoke City FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bristol City's home advantage at Ashton Gate and four-point lead in the EFL Championship table—13th on 59 points versus Stoke City's 17th-place 55—position them as trader consensus favorites at 48.5% implied probability in this final-day mid-table clash, despite matching 13 clean sheets and recent offensive struggles. Both sides enter winless in their last four and five outings respectively, with Bristol losing four of five home games and Stoke suffering six straight away defeats, exacerbated by injuries sidelining Bristol's Max Bird, Rob Atkinson, and Luke McNally alongside Stoke's Tomas Rigo, Aaron Cresswell, Bosun Lawal, and others. The near-even split reflects a closely contested matchup with draw pricing at 25%, tempered by mixed head-to-head history including Stoke's 5-1 win in November.

markets.question: Will John Thune be the next Senate Majority Leader?

This market will remain open until January 3, 2027, at which point it will resolve based on the first announcement of the next Senate Majority Leader from whichever party holds the majority in the U.S. Senate.

If no Majority is established and a leader is not announced by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Chuck Schumer at 43.5% as next Senate Majority Leader after the November 2026 midterms, mirroring aggregated prediction market odds implying roughly 50-52% chance of Democrats flipping the Republican 53-47 majority amid competitive battlegrounds like Maine, North Carolina, Ohio, and Georgia. John Thune lags at 30% amid escalating GOP backlash over his leadership, including the recent 76-day DHS shutdown—resolved via Republican-only reconciliation—and refusals to advance House-passed SAVE America Act for voter citizenship verification or Central Bank Digital Currency bans, fueling calls for his removal from hardline conservatives. Recent Democratic fundraising surges and an elections task force launch bolster flip prospects, while Schatz's 12.5% reflects speculation on Democratic leadership alternatives.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former U.S. Rep. Ben McAdams leads trader consensus at 73% implied probability for Utah's 1st Congressional District Democratic primary on June 23, driven by his top polling (36% in late March Data for Progress survey), superior fundraising ($1.5 million raised, $863,000 cash on hand), and prior name recognition from his 2019-2021 House service and Salt Lake County mayoralty. State Sen. Nate Blouin's 25% share reflects progressive endorsements like Bernie Sanders' but is weighed down by mid-April resurfacing of decade-old offensive social media posts mocking Mormons and joking about sexual assault. Liban Mohamed's narrow ranked-choice convention endorsement win on April 25 boosted activists but failed to sway broader primary odds, as delegates differ from voters; low-fundraised challengers like Caroline Gleich trail. No new polls have emerged post-convention.

markets.question: Fed rate hike in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the Fed's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 8-9, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the Fed has released its rate change decision following its December meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve's April 28-29, 2026 FOMC decision to hold the federal funds target range steady at 3.50%-3.75% for a third straight meeting drives the 82.5% market-implied probability of no rate hike in 2026 on Polymarket, reflecting trader consensus on sustained policy accommodation. Despite March CPI inflation accelerating to 3.3% year-over-year—the highest since 2024—and unemployment dipping to 4.3%, Chair Powell's April 29 press conference affirmed the current stance as appropriate to foster progress toward dual mandate goals, with the March dot plot projecting a median 3.4% fed funds rate by year-end 2026. Resilient labor data tempers tightening risks, while 10-year Treasury yields hover around 4.4%. Key catalysts include April CPI release on May 12 and the June 16-17 FOMC.

markets.question: Will Roberto Vannacci be the next Prime Minister of Italy?

To count for resolution, the individual must be officially appointed and sworn in as Prime Minister of Italy. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister takes office by December 31, 2028, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Italy; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Giorgia Meloni leads trader consensus at 46.5% implied probability as next Prime Minister, buoyed by Fratelli d'Italia's persistent poll lead around 28-29% in late April surveys despite a March judicial reform referendum defeat that narrowed centre-right coalitions to near parity with centre-left blocs. Recent SWG and Ixè polls show PD under Elly Schlein gaining to 22-23% and M5S led by Giuseppe Conte at 12-13%, fueling their shares at 12% and 9.5%, while fragmented opposition dynamics boost Genoa Mayor Silvia Salis at 8.9% amid her rising profile as a unifying progressive figure. Far-right challenger Roberto Vannacci's 11.7% reflects his Futuro Nazionale party's 3% support post-League split, heightening right-wing vote risks ahead of 2027 elections or potential snap polls.

markets.question: Will the Republicans win the 2028 US Presidential Election?
markets.description:
The 2028 United States presidential election will be held on Tuesday, November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the political party whose candidate is elected the next President of the United States in the 2028 election.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same party. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same party by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's approval ratings plunged to 34-38% in late April polls—the lowest of his second term—amid high gas prices from the Iran conflict and broader economic headwinds, fueling trader bets on Democratic gains. Polymarket consensus prices Democrats at 60.5% to win the 2028 presidential election, reflecting historical midterm losses for the incumbent party ahead of November 2026 contests where Democrats lead generic ballots by eight points and hold strong odds for House (87%) and Senate (55%) control. Term limits bar Trump's re-run, exposing Republicans to an open primary led by JD Vance (19%) against Democratic frontrunners like Gavin Newsom (18%), with swing states pivotal for Electoral College math and outcomes highly sensitive to midterm results.

markets.question: LoL: Kiwoom DRX vs Nongshim Red Force (BO3) - LCK Rounds 1-2
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Kiwoom DRX and Nongshim Red Force in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for May 2 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Kiwoom DRX" if Kiwoom DRX win the match against Nongshim Red Force.

This market will resolve to "Nongshim Red Force" if Nongshim Red Force win the match against Kiwoom DRX.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Will Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) hit (HIGH) $148 Week of April 27 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of April 27 2026, any 1-minute candle for Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.ABNB%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.ABNB%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will OpenAI’s market cap be $1.5T or greater at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on OpenAI's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by December 31, 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on OpenAI’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

markets.question: Evo Morales arrested by May 31
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Evo Morales, former President of Bolivia, is arrested or detained by law enforcement between market creation and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Temporary holding at a detention facility while awaiting a judge’s decision on whether to grant a detention warrant qualifies, so long as they are held by law enforcement in an official capacity during that time.

A qualifying arrest/detention includes:
* Being taken into physical custody by law enforcement authorities (including federal, state, local, military, or international law enforcement)
* Voluntarily surrendering to law enforcement authorities in response to an arrest warrant
* Being formally booked or processed following detention
* Being handcuffed by a state authority and taken to a police station
* Surrendering at a police station or courthouse with their attorney
* Being placed under house arrest or electronic monitoring

The following scenarios will NOT qualify as an arrest/detention
* An arrest warrant being issued but not executed
* Being questioned or interviewed without arrest
* Being named in an indictment without arrest

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the arresting government and law enforcement; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Under center-right President Rodrigo Paz, who took office in November 2025 following MAS defeats, no verified operations or raids targeting Morales have occurred in the past 30 days, with Foreign Minister Fernando Aramayo recently noting his unresolved judicial issues bar passport renewal without personal appearance. Trader consensus at 84.5% "No" reflects this stalled momentum, political risks of unrest, and historical difficulties apprehending him before the May 31 deadline, absent a sudden escalation.

markets.question: Game Handicap: EDG (-3.5) vs SYGaming (+3.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Honor of Kings Round 2 match between SYGaming and EDward Gaming in the Honor of Kings Challenger Cup Stage 1, initially scheduled for May 1 at 2:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "EDward Gaming" if EDward Gaming wins 4 or more games than SYGaming in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "SYGaming". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/honorofkings/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/honorofkings/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Honor of Kings: SYGaming vs EDward Gaming (BO7) - Honor of Kings Challenger Cup Stage 1
events.description:
This market refers to the Honor of Kings Round 2 match between SYGaming and EDward Gaming in the Honor of Kings Challenger Cup Stage 1, initially scheduled for May 1 at 2:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "SYGaming" if SYGaming win the match against EDward Gaming.

This market will resolve to "EDward Gaming" if EDward Gaming win the match against SYGaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/honorofkings/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/honorofkings/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: EDward Gaming and SYGaming advanced to Round 2 of the Honor of Kings Challenger Cup 2026 Stage 1 single-elimination bracket via contrasting Bo5 wins: EDG's efficient 3-1 defeat of lower-tier QingXun-QianYuan on April 25, and SYG's resilient 3-2 comeback over blessing-aided ZDC on April 26. EDG carries KPL pedigree with recent mixed TY TianJiao Cup showings (wins over Team WE and Hero JiuJing, losses to Wolves and Weibo Gaming), supported by stable roster including junglers XiaLing and YunLi. SYGaming rides KGL Spring group stage momentum as second-place finishers. In this pivotal Bo7, trader focus centers on EDG's macro execution versus SYG's upset potential through extended series endurance and draft flexibility.

markets.question: Will Eurozone annual GDP growth in 2026 be at least 7.0%?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the estimation of Euro Area (Eurozone) annual GDP growth for the full year of 2026 (% change), based on seasonally and calendar adjusted quarterly data, as reported in the "GDP and employment flash estimates for the fourth quarter of 2026" flash release for Q4 of 2026, scheduled to be released in January 2027.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/web/main/news/euro-indicators

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If no data for the Euro Area GDP growth rate for the full year of 2026 is included in this release, this market will resolve according to the Euro Area GDP growth rate for Q4 2026, as compared to the same quarter in the previous year. If no data is released for either the full year or fourth quarter of 2026 by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter, as compared to the same quarter in the previous year.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced flash GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release of the specified report will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a modest 2026 Eurozone annual GDP growth trajectory, with 1.0-2.0% holding a 36% implied probability ahead of 0-1.0% at 29.5%, reflecting closely contested sentiment after Eurostat's April 30 flash data showed Q1 quarter-on-quarter expansion slowing to just 0.1% versus 0.2% consensus, alongside April inflation surging to 3% on Middle East war-driven energy shocks. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.
